textByHash.6b4868388e049c6c8f5a9dd2c989ccf8f8a31fcd: That's right. Why do you ask?
textByHash.3d4fe5a1631fc145e95b759761dbec64a34ba602: A vampyre? This must have been a very long time ago.
textByHash.430e0eff6b3f1457192cae24c637a404fa6f6cc4: Oh wow, that is a long time! You're talking about a time before Great Kourend even existed!
textByHash.f985a66d68dd02f5411b47fafdbd92c7cd89fb67: Hmm... You say this vampyre was in pursuit of Yonrith?
textByHash.5b314740adf2150bb6af9eb41b39d51a16d8bd3f: Well... it could be nothing, but there's this monument in Lovakengj called the Burning Man. Apparently, it represents the defeat of an ancient evil. Could be a good place to start?
textByHash.24bda2e64f1576d904bc1f250f59beb23dfce3fc: You'll find it in western Lovakengj. It's pretty hard to miss!
textByHash.3cd0798a985c5a1e3f0663908b42932844e8862d: We've had this conversation before, haven't we? If I were you, I'd check out the Burning Man monument in western Lovakengj. Apparently it represents the defeat of an ancient evil.
textByHash.65f3a302e295235327cac28a9bf89dd29556ff30: I'm helping with the dig. I'm an expert on Third Age architecture.
textByHash.8c42337bd8748145d784ab2c3d4ba8a7d5c2ac72: In the Third Age, this was a great city. Look at these giant walls! They put Varrock to shame!
textByHash.6ebf0f2d7de824d6d026cb28476c5fbf78821b15: Hmph. I don't think it will look this good when it's been buried in the ground for three thousand years!
textByHash.1a9fcd4a50218b063ea8e1bcd2eb6713651e7669: But the Third Age was a time of destruction, when the gods were violently at war.
textByHash.446813cde01564ca26064fcce17e801bfebf10f5: Many great civilizations were destroyed then.
textByHash.ba5f96d380c0792c99e1e7c67cbb461f58d92cdb: Ah, so you've found the ruins of Uzer.
textByHash.cc141e3b994653bd6261d7adadfd5ca52ca2d596: AI wrote that letter to my late husband when he was exploring there.
textByHash.a683277a42cea82d04c7af3b30a075295ec34ce2: that was a great city as well, but the museum could only fund one excavation and this one was closer to home.
textByHash.320fe64f5c4fb6eef20ea43d813e9cfa5c446327: If you're interested in his expedition, the notes he made are in the library in the Exam Centre.
textByHash.17c8a6466e1c42c1f6ad17c407eaa056c153e92d: They're on a bookcase in the Exam Centre.
textByHash.70b74f569d5baa75a97b26da1f52db0cdee41a68: That statuette was the only thing we had to show from that expedition.
textByHash.c6f755bbb9f93e4bf948984e28bb12a11f2c576c: It was very worn, but you can still make out a lot of detail. The Uzerians were expert sculptors. It's a pity we only have that small example.
textByHash.8068b7a6cfa6cc63240cafea1f0aff5605b84ced: Now it's on display in the Varrock museum.
